steps.title: Combine the functions f(x) and g(x)
steps.content:
Substitute the functions into $h(x) = f(x) + g(x)$:
$$h(x) = \frac{1}{5}x - 9 + \frac{4}{5}x + 27$$
Combine the $x$-terms: $\frac{1}{5}x + \frac{4}{5}x = x$
Combine the constants: $-9 + 27 = 18$
So $h(x) = x + 18$

steps.title: Set h(x) = 0 and solve for x
steps.content:
The $x$-intercept occurs where $y = 0$:
$$0 = x + 18$$
$$x = -18$$
The $x$-intercept is at $(-18, 0)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
